marketState: neutral — extraordinary heat surge colliding with bearish seasonality and ample storage surplus
summary: Aug 2026 front-month at $3.176 sits modestly above the current fair-value midpoint of $2.96 (SLIGHTLY OVER) but well within the $2.46–$3.46 band, so there is no acute overextension in either direction. The dominant near-term force is an extraordinary heat event: demand for the next 1–7 days is 45% above the 5-year average and above the 5-year maximum (aboveMax=true), the 8–16D window is also above max (+25% vs 5y), and the trend is still surging — 195.0 Bcf/d, +15.1 in 24h, and +54% above 5y normal. Against this, the 4-week seasonal pattern is sharply bearish (only 19% historical probability of a higher close, typical move −1.8%), storage sits +151 Bcf above the 5-year average with a small bearish injection surprise this week (+6 Bcf vs forecast), and most technicals (daily, weekly, 1h) are in a bearish EMA structure (50 below 200). Managed-money net is at the 18th percentile of its 5-year range — near maximum short crowding — which is a contrarian bull signal and creates genuine short-squeeze risk that limits the appeal of new shorts at current levels. The futures curve shows near-month backwardation (Aug above Sep by $0.053), consistent with a transient weather premium, while deep winter contango (Jan 2027 at $4.358) reflects seasonal tightening expectations rather than a structural front-month bull case.

longTerm.rationale: Seasonality (19% chance higher in 4 weeks, −1.8% typical move) and storage (+151 Bcf above 5y, bearish injection surprise) both argue for a SHORT over 3–12 weeks, but managed-money at the 18th percentile of its 5-year net represents near-maximum bearish crowding — shorting into a crowded position while extreme heat is still in the 8–16D window is a poor risk/reward. The better entry is either a weather-driven spike toward the $3.30–$3.46 upper-band area (sell into strength) or, alternatively, waiting for the heat to break and shorting a retest of $3.00–$3.05 from above once demand normalises.
longTerm.invalidation: Storage surplus erodes to below the 5-year average on multiple consecutive above-consensus draw weeks, or managed-money net rises above the 40th percentile of the 5-year range, signalling that the short squeeze has cleared the bearish overhang.
longTerm.trigger: Initiate SHORT on rally to $3.30–$3.46 (entry ~$3.35, stop $3.52, TP1 $3.05, TP2 $2.80, R:R ~1:1.9) — OR wait for heat demand to drop back below the 5y maximum and short a retest of $3.00–$3.05 from above (entry ~$3.03, stop $3.20, TP1 $2.80, TP2 $2.60, R:R ~1:2.0).

shortTerm.rationale: Both the 1–7D and 8–16D demand windows are above the 5-year maximum — a rare heat event that typically forces the market to price in additional weather premium toward the upper fair-value band at $3.46. The 1h RSI at 31.2 is approaching oversold territory, and the 5m MACD is already positive, suggesting near-term selling exhaustion. With $0.28 of upside to TP2 against $0.13 of downside to the stop, the weather catalyst delivers an asymmetric short-term setup.
shortTerm.invalidation: Daily close below $3.05 (within striking distance of current fair value midpoint, implying the weather premium has been fully stripped); or 1–7D demand reverts to below the 5-year maximum.

keyDrivers: Demand 1–7D at +45% vs 5y average and above the 5y maximum (aboveMax=true); 8–16D also above max — rare double-window heat event
Demand trend surging: 195.0 Bcf/d, +15.1 in 24h, +54% vs 5y normal — still accelerating at snapshot time
Managed money net at 18th percentile of 5y range — near maximum short crowding, creating short-squeeze risk and an effective floor
Seasonality sharply bearish: 4-week probability only 19% higher, typical move −1.8%, though reliability is flagged Low (51pp spread)
Storage +151 Bcf above 5y average with a bearish injection surprise (+6 Bcf vs forecast) — structural headwind once heat fades
risks: Heat breaks earlier than the 8–16D window implies, stripping the weather premium and turning the short-term LONG into a loss before TP1 is reached

COT short-squeeze: managed money at the 18th percentile means any sustained bullish headline could trigger rapid covering, driving price well above the $3.46 upper band and forcing early exit of any future short
Low seasonal reliability (51pp 10–90% spread) means the bearish 4-week pattern could easily fail — a late-summer heat dome could hold prices elevated through September
